title: The Armies Rush Toward Alubarna
description: On pages 1–3, this part focuses on the armies rush toward alubarna, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Armies Rush Toward Alubarna. The rebellion and royal army rush toward Alubarna as Vivi’s group searches for a way to stop the war before it starts. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That doesn't look like any key I've ever seen!

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: W... water...! I'm saved, aren't I...!?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: What was he doing inside a bamanadile!?

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: I thought I was a goner for the longest time!

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: Heh heh... You've underestimated me, Crocodile!

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: I used the last of my strength to make a Dora Dora Ball to hide safely inside, you see...!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Even for me, this was a most brilliant plan, if I must say so myself!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: Hm? What's this odd key stuck to my Dora Dora Ball...?

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: And a key... Ah, of course!

dialogue.speaker: Crocodile
dialogue.text: I've now grasped the current situation, I believe...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: So you're Mr. 3... Hand that key over already.

summary: This part develops the armies rush toward alubarna and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
